title: Interstitial Lung Disease

patient.name: Susan Park
patient.persona: former teacher, never smoker, worried about progressive breathlessness

chief_complaint: I get out of breath just walking to the bathroom now. I've had a dry cough for 6 months. My fingers feel different too.

physical_exam.General: Tachypneic at rest, clubbing of fingers and toes
physical_exam.Lungs: Fine end-inspiratory crackles (Velcro rales) at both bases, no wheezing
physical_exam.Cardiac: Regular, loud P2 component of S2, no murmurs
physical_exam.Extremities: Digital clubbing, no cyanosis, no edema

hidden_data.abg_exercise: pH 7.42, PaCO2 34, PaO2 55, SaO2 84% (significant desaturation with 6-minute walk)
hidden_data.pft: FVC 58% predicted, FEV1 62% predicted, FEV1/FVC 0.88 (restrictive pattern), TLC 55%, DLCO 40% predicted
hidden_data.cxr: Bilateral reticular opacities, predominantly in lower lobes. Reduced lung volumes. No pleural effusion.
hidden_data.hrct: Peripheral, subpleural honeycombing with traction bronchiectasis, predominantly basilar. Ground-glass opacities in upper lobes. Pattern consistent with UIP (Usual Interstitial Pneumonia).
hidden_data.autoimmune: ANA negative, RF negative, anti-CCP negative. Scl-70 and Jo-1 antibodies negative.

correct_diagnosis: Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF) - Usual Interstitial Pneumonia (UIP) Pattern
key_learning_points: IPF = progressive fibrotic lung disease of unknown cause, UIP pattern on HRCT, age >50, poor prognosis
Classic triad: Progressive dyspnea + dry cough + inspiratory crackles (Velcro rales) + clubbing
PFTs show restrictive pattern: decreased FVC, normal or increased FEV1/FVC ratio, decreased DLCO
HRCT is diagnostic gold standard: Honeycombing + traction bronchiectasis in peripheral, basilar distribution
Antifibrotic therapy (Pirfenidone, Nintedanib) slows disease progression. Median survival 3-5 years without transplant.

patient_responses.onset: It started about a year ago. I noticed I couldn't keep up with my grandkids. Now I can't walk across the room without stopping.
patient_responses.cough: Dry, hacking cough. Nothing comes up. It's worse when I try to take a deep breath. I've had it for months.
patient_responses.risk_factors: I never smoked. I was a teacher. No exposure to chemicals or birds. No family history of lung disease.
patient_responses.medications: Just vitamins. No prescription medications. I tried cough syrup but it didn't help.
patient_responses.other_symptoms: I've lost about 15 pounds without trying. I'm always tired. My joints ache sometimes.

hints.history: Progressive dyspnea over months to years. Dry cough. Ask about occupational exposures, medications, connective tissue disease symptoms.
hints.examination: Velcro rales (fine inspiratory crackles) are characteristic. Clubbing suggests chronic hypoxia. Look for signs of connective tissue disease.
hints.labs: PFTs with DLCO - restrictive pattern with reduced diffusion capacity is hallmark. 6-minute walk test for desaturation. Autoimmune panel to rule out CTD-ILD.
hints.imaging: HRCT is diagnostic - UIP pattern (honeycombing, traction bronchiectasis, peripheral/basilar predominance) = IPF without biopsy.

feedback.correct_message: Excellent diagnosis! You correctly identified IPF from the progressive dyspnea, Velcro rales, clubbing, restrictive PFTs, and classic UIP pattern on HRCT. Antifibrotic therapy and transplant evaluation are the next steps.
feedback.partially_correct_message: Good thinking! This is ILD with UIP pattern, not NSIP or other variants. The HRCT shows classic honeycombing and traction bronchiectasis in a peripheral/basilar distribution, which is diagnostic for IPF.
feedback.incorrect_message: The clinical picture (progressive dyspnea, clubbing, Velcro rales, restrictive PFTs) with HRCT showing peripheral honeycombing is classic for IPF/UIP. IPF is a specific diagnosis requiring antifibrotic therapy, not just 'pulmonary fibrosis'. Refer to pulmonology and transplant center.
clinical_pearls: Velcro rales (fine end-inspiratory crackles) are heard in >90% of IPF patients
DLCO is often disproportionately reduced compared to FVC in IPF
Antifibrotic therapy slows FVC decline by 50% but does not reverse fibrosis

pft.comment: Restrictive pattern with reduced DLCO confirms physiological severity and tracks progression

hrct.comment: UIP pattern on HRCT is diagnostic for IPF without surgical lung biopsy per 2018 ATS/ERS guidelines

abg_exercise.comment: Exercise desaturation indicates severity and need for supplemental oxygen

autoimmune.comment: Must rule out CTD-ILD which has different treatment (immunosuppression)

cxr.comment: Shows reticular opacities but HRCT is needed for definitive diagnosis

missing_tests: 6-minute walk test - Objective measure of functional impairment and oxygen needs
feedback.attending_note: Doctor, this is classic IPF. Start supplemental oxygen for SpO2 <88% at rest or with exertion. Begin Nintedanib 150mg BID or Pirfenidone. Vaccinate (influenza, pneumococcal, COVID-19). Refer for pulmonary rehabilitation and lung transplant evaluation (age <70, no major comorbidities). Discuss prognosis honestly but compassionately. Median survival 3-5 years.
